title: digitalis purpurea f. nevadensis
intro: Digitalis purpurea f. nevadensis is a variety of the foxglove plant, specifically a form of Digitalis purpurea native to the state of Nevada in the United States. It contains the cardiac glycosides digitoxin and digoxin, which have been used for centuries to treat various heart conditions due to their ability to increase the force of heart contractions. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea f. nevadensis are primarily used to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation, congestive heart failure, and certain types of arrhythmia, and it is also used to increase cardiac output in patients with weakened hearts. Herbal preparations of Digitalis purpurea f. nevadensis are typically made from the dried leaves and flowers, and are available in various forms including tinctures, capsules, and extracts.
